SourcesMetadata.AuditScope: Discover authoritative documentation for PropFuel's API surface, particularly the data export file-feed integration
SourcesMetadata.FindingSummary: PropFuel's official documentation is dispersed across a support portal with limited machine-readable specs. No OpenAPI/Swagger specification, Postman collection, or comprehensive API reference discovered. All documented sources are HTML-based help articles with operational (not technical) focus.

Sources.Title: PropFuel Connectors & Fields (Help Center Category)
Sources.Description: Official help center category covering 57+ articles on connector setup, field mapping, and integration instructions. Primary official documentation for third-party system integrations.
Sources.AccessNotes: Publicly accessible; no authentication required. Full category listing verified 2026-06-08.
Sources.RefreshFrequency: Appears to be updated regularly (product updates referenced)

Sources.Title: PropFuel Connectors: How to Integrate PropFuel With Other Systems
Sources.Description: Official help article explaining PropFuel's integration architecture. Covers 50+ connectors, field mapping, bidirectional data sync, and general integration setup. Directs to individual connector docs for specific API details.
Sources.AccessNotes: Publicly accessible. This is the landing article for all integration documentation.
Sources.Note: Does NOT contain specific API endpoint documentation; points to per-connector articles instead

Sources.Title: PropFuel Help Center (Main Portal)
Sources.Description: Official support portal hosting all PropFuel documentation. Organized into: Getting Started (23 articles), Connectors & Fields (57 articles), Email Campaigns (31), Website Engagement (9), SMS Campaigns (10), Analysis & Reporting (16), Campaign Guides (15), and Additional Resources (31).
Sources.AccessNotes: Publicly accessible portal. No developer/API section discovered.
Sources.Gap: No dedicated API reference, developer portal, or machine-readable schema available at this location

Sources.Title: iMIS & iMIS EMS (Cloud) — Connector Documentation
Sources.Description: Specific connector documentation for iMIS integration. Specifies three required credentials: Username, Password, and API URL. Documents the GetParty REST API endpoint. References IQA (Inquiry/Query Approach) for data pulling and custom table write-backs.
Sources.AccessNotes: Publicly accessible. Limited to iMIS specifics; shows pattern for other connectors.
Sources.Note: Write-back details deferred to 'talk to your account manager' — not documented in help center

Sources.Title: PropFuel Marketing Website
Sources.Description: Official product website. Describes PropFuel as an AI-powered member insights and engagement platform. Mentions integration with major AMS platforms (Nimble AMS, Fonteva, iMIS, YourMembership, MemberClicks). Focuses on business value, not technical specifications.
Sources.AccessNotes: Publicly accessible; marketing-focused, not technical documentation.
Sources.Gap: No technical API documentation on marketing site

Sources.Title: PropFuel Capabilities
Sources.Description: High-level overview of PropFuel's feature set: Insights Engine, Automation Engine (70+ templates), Engagement Engine (email/web/SMS), AI-powered personalization, bidirectional AMS sync.
Sources.AccessNotes: Publicly accessible; marketing content, not technical reference.
Sources.Gap: No API endpoint or integration technical details

Sources.Title: Types of Workflow Actions in PropFuel
Sources.Description: Help article documenting PropFuel's workflow action types and automation capabilities. Relevant for understanding what actions connectors can trigger.

SourceGaps.Reason: No OpenAPI/Swagger specification, GraphQL SDL, or Postman collection discovered in public sources. PropFuel does not appear to publish a formal machine-readable contract for third-party API consumers.
SourceGaps.Workaround: Connector must implement discovery dynamically (via introspection endpoints if available) or rely on human-compiled help articles for endpoint/field information.

SourceGaps.Reason: No comprehensive API endpoint documentation. Individual connector help articles (like iMIS) show examples, but a canonical API reference is absent. The data export file-feed context (provided by user) is the most specific documented API surface.
SourceGaps.Workaround: Connector must derive endpoints from help articles, user-provided context, or reverse-engineer from live system behavior if credentials available.

SourceGaps.Reason: Help articles do not document request/response payload schemas, field types, constraints (max length, required vs optional), or value enumerations.
SourceGaps.Workaround: Schema inference from live data samples or live introspection at runtime.

SourceGaps.Reason: Individual connectors specify their own auth requirements (OAuth, API keys, username/password). No unified AuthN/AuthZ spec or scope/permission documentation for PropFuel's own API.
SourceGaps.Workaround: Per-connector help articles document auth details; data export context specifies Bearer token. Assume each connector/surface has its own auth scheme.

SourceGaps.Reason: No documented error codes, HTTP status interpretations, or recommended retry strategies.
SourceGaps.Workaround: Implement standard REST error handling; derive specifics from live error responses.

SourceGaps.Reason: No documented rate limits, batch operation limits, or request/second budgets.
SourceGaps.Workaround: Implement adaptive rate limiting based on live 429 / rate-limit headers; start conservative.

SourceGaps.Reason: No documented pagination strategies (offset, cursor, keyset) or default/max page sizes.
SourceGaps.Workaround: Infer from live responses; user-provided data export context suggests file-based pagination (list/download by filename).

ResearchSummary.DiscoveryMethod: Systematic web search + WebFetch of PropFuel's official portal, marketing site, and connector help articles
AvailableSources: Official help center (HTML articles, no machine-readable schema)
User-provided data export API context (JSON file-feed specifics, highest technical detail)
AbsentSources: GitHub public repositories with API docs
ResearchSummary.CredentialRequirement: All public documentation is credential-free and accessible. Live API introspection would require vendor credentials (bearer token for data export, AMS-specific credentials for connectors).
RecommendedNextSteps: Use user-provided data export context as primary technical source (most specific documented API surface)
Request official API documentation from PropFuel support if available (may be gated or require partnership agreement)
Implement runtime discovery/introspection if PropFuel data export endpoints support schema introspection
Plan connector architecture to support dynamic field discovery at sync time (custom column capture via full-record pass-through)
